This is a game to make any gap-fill task or cloze texts (reading tasks with gaps) more exciting. You need an envelope full of small bits of paper. Chop up a few pages of scrap paper for this. Students compete against one another in pairs. Give each pair a bunch of small bits of paper to keep on their table. Explain that they are going to compete against their partner to win as many bits of paper as possible. Each one is worth one point. You are going to read some sentences with gaps in. When there's a gap you'll say 'beep'. They must write the beeped out word on a bit of paper without their partner seeing. Set a 5 second time limit and then say, "Okay, turn over". Both of the students turn over their papers at the same time. If both are correct, leave them on the table to accumulate. If both are wrong, leave them on the table to accumulate. When one student is right and the other wrong, the student who is right wins all the accumulated cards that have gathered, and the piles start again on the next question. This is great for practising prepositions, such as the time prepositions in/on/at which students often make mistakes with. - My birthday is ____ "beep" November 22nd. - I have English class _____ "beep" Wednesdays and Fridays. - School starts _____ "beep" 8.30 am. - I'm going to Cornwall ____ "beep" the holidays. When you have done ten or so sentences, stop the game and get students to count how many bits of paper they won. Rather than having just one or two winners in the class after a game, the great thing about this one is that half the class win as each pair has a winner. (Thanks to Nicole Taylor for sharing this one in a training session a few years ago.) By Jo Budden First published 2009 [the girl is swimming in the ocean] mm, this is so hard to get! There's a hole like an arm in one. oh, look at that they're kissing in the water. Why does ice float? (Lansing State Journal 4/8/98) In order to answer this question we first have to understand why anything floats. This question is easiest to understand in the case of water. When you put a bunch of marbles into a bowl of water, you can see the level of the water rise. In fact , the marbles are pushing the water out of the way, that is, displacing the water. The marbles sink because they are more dense than the water. In order for an object to float, it must be able to displace a weight of fluid equal to its own weight. The amount of water that is displaced by a marble weighs less than the marble, so the marble sinks. As we discussed last week, water expands when it freezes, becoming ice. So when an ice cube floats in a bowl of water, the ice displaces an amount of water equal to its weight, so it floats. This property of ice is very important since the top of a lake can freeze without sinking. If the ice sunk, then the entire lake would fill up with ice and all the fish would be frozen. One day, the white octopus met a little fish who was very sad. The white octopus asked the little fish, "Why are you sad, little fish?" The little fish said, "I am sad because I have no friends to play with." The white octopus felt bad for the little fish. He said, "Don't worry, little fish. I will be your friend, and we can play together!" So, the white octopus and the little fish played together all day long. They had so much fun. The little fish was not sad anymore. They became best friends forever. The moral of the story is: always be kind and help others, and you will make many friends. Title: How to Change Alarm Sound on Your iPhone Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through changing the alarm sound on your iPhone. This can be useful if you're tired of the same old alert tone or if you want to customize your device to better suit your preferences. By following these steps, you'll learn how to navigate through your phone's settings, select a new sound from various options, and preview the change before saving it. Let's get started! Step 1: Unlock your iPhone and open the "Clock" app The Clock app is typically found on your home screen or within the Utilities folder. Tapping on this icon will launch the application, which allows you to manage time-related tasks such as setting alarms, timers, stopwatches, and world clocks. Step 2: Navigate to the "Alarm" tab Once the Clock app has opened, look at the bottom menu bar and tap on the "Alarm" button (the clock with a bell icon). This action displays all existing alarms on your device along with an option to add a new one (+) located at the upper right corner. Key Tip: If you have multiple alarms set up, ensure that you've selected the correct alarm whose sound you wish to modify. You can identify it by its label, scheduled time, or recurrence pattern. Step 3: Select the alarm you wish to edit Tap on the specific alarm that needs its sound changed. Doing so reveals several configurable parameters associated with that particular alarm, including the alarm name, repeat schedule, snooze duration, and most importantly, the sound selection. Step 4: Access the sound options After selecting the target alarm, locate the "Sound" section under its configuration panel. It usually contains default sounds like "Chime," "Birdsong," "Classical," etc., followed by ''Pick a song''. To access more sound choices, tap on "Sounds." Guideline: The Sounds list comprises built-in tones categorized into sections like Classic, Acoustic, Electronic, and others based on their genre or mood. Feel free to explore them until you find something appealing. Step 5: Choose a new sound Scroll through the available categories and listen to previews of different sounds by tapping on them individually. Once you find a suitable replacement, remember its position or name since we'll need to go back to it shortly. Important Note: Some users might prefer using a personalized tune instead of the provided selections. For such cases, refer to Step 6 below. Step 6: Set a custom song as your alarm tone (Optional) If you didn't find any preset sound satisfying, consider choosing a song from your music library as follows: a. After tapping "Sound", scroll down and hit "Pick a song". b. Wait for iTunes to load your entire Music Library. c. Browse through your collection, then select a track. d. Confirm your choice by hitting "Back" twice to return to the alarm editing interface. Step 7: Preview and save changes Having chosen your preferred sound, press the "Back" button once to reach the main alarm editing page. Listen to the newly selected sound by ensuring that the switch next to "Sound" is toggled ON. If satisfied, tap "Save" at the top right corner to store the modifications made. Congratulations! You've successfully changed the alarm sound on your iPhone. From now onwards, expect a refreshing audio cue whenever this alarm goes off. I asked you if you do all you height answer is yes or no. Title: How to Get Your Guinea Pig to Eat a Treat Out of Your Hand Introduction: Guinea pigs are social creatures that can be trained to eat treats out of your hand. This bonding experience is not only fun but also beneficial for their emotional well-being. Training your guinea pig to eat from your hand requires patience, consistency, and positive reinforcement. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to accomplish this goal. Step 1: Choose an Appropriate Treat Select a healthy treat that your guinea pig enjoys. Some good options include small pieces of bell pepper, carrot, or cucumber. Avoid sugary or processed foods, as these can lead to health issues. The chosen treat should be easy to hold and handle. Why this step matters: By selecting a favorite treat, you increase the likelihood of your guinea pig associating your hand with something pleasant, which encourages them to eat from your hand more readily. Step 2: Create a Positive Environment Place your guinea pig in a calm, quiet space free from distractions. Make sure they have enough room to move around comfortably. A familiar environment will help reduce stress and make training easier. Key tip: Never force your guinea pig to take a treat from your hand. Instead, let them approach you at their own pace. Forcing interaction may cause fear or aggression. Step 3: Build Trust Spend time near your guinea pig while offering them treats from a short distance away. Gradually decrease the gap between your hand and their mouth until they feel comfortable taking treats directly from your fingers. Guideline: Be patient during this process; building trust takes time. If your guinea pig seems uncomfortable, return to a previous stage and try again later. Step 4: Present the Treat Correctly Hold the treat between your thumb and index finger, keeping your other three fingers extended and relaxed. Position your hand so that the treat faces upwards towards your guinea pig's nose. This orientation allows them to recognize the food easily and reduces the risk of injury. Why this step matters: Proper presentation ensures safety and makes it simpler for your guinea pig to accept the treat from your hand. Step 5: Encourage Acceptance Gently tap the treat against your guinea pig's lips or nose to prompt them to investigate further. Speak softly and calmly throughout this process, using words they associate with positive experiences (e.g., "yummy," "good boy/girl"). Key tip: Refrain from making sudden movements or loud noises, as these actions could scare off your guinea pig. Step 6: Reinforce Good Behavior If your guinea pig eats the treat from your hand, offer praise and additional rewards. Petting them gently or giving another treat after successful interactions reinforces positive behavior and strengthens your bond. Why this step matters: Reinforcing good behavior increases the chances of repeat performances, solidifying the association between your hand and tasty snacks. Step 7: Practice Regularly Dedicate time daily to practice these steps until your guinea pig confidently eats treats from your hand without hesitation. Consistency is crucial for success. Conclusion: Training your guinea pig to eat treats from your hand fosters a closer relationship between you two while providing mental stimulation for your pet. With patience, persistence, and plenty of love, even the most timid guinea pig can learn to enjoy this special bonding activity. " Are you behind the ball? Tutorial: How to Close Tabs on Samsung Galaxy S3 The Samsung Galaxy S3 is a powerful smartphone that allows users to have multiple tabs open at once in its built-in internet browser. However, having too many tabs open can slow down your phone's performance and consume unnecessary data. This tutorial will guide you through the process of closing tabs on your Samsung Galaxy S3. Step 1: Open the Internet Browser To begin, locate and tap on the Internet icon on your home screen or app drawer. This will launch the default internet browser on your device. Key Tip: If you cannot find the Internet icon, try swiping down from the top of the screen to access the notification panel, then select the Internet icon from there. Step 2: Access the Tab View Once the internet browser is opened, tap on the tab view button located at the upper right corner of the screen. The tab view button looks like two small overlapping rectangles. This action will display all currently open tabs as thumbnails. By viewing all the tabs simultaneously, you can efficiently manage which ones to keep open or close. Guideline: Keep only essential tabs open to optimize battery life, reduce data usage, and improve overall device performance. Step 3: Identify Unnecessary Tabs Take some time to review the displayed tabs. Determine which tabs are no longer needed by asking yourself if their content remains relevant to your current browsing session. For instance, if you previously researched a recipe but finished cooking, consider closing those related tabs since they won't be required any further. Step 4: Swipe Away Unwanted Tabs Now that you've identified unneeded tabs, it's time to close them. Locate an unwanted tab within the tab view, place your finger on it, and gently swipe it towards the left or right edge of the screen. As you do this, the tab will disappear off the side of the screen, signifying successful closure. By physically interacting with the tabs using gestures, you develop muscle memory, making future tab management more intuitive. Step 5: Confirm Closure After successfully removing a tab via swiping, take note of the updated tab count shown near the bottom of the screen. Make sure the number decreases after each closed tab, confirming proper execution. Additionally, observe how individual tab thumbnails vanish when removed, ensuring visual feedback reinforcing correct actions taken. Key Tips: * To quickly close all open tabs, press and hold the tab view button (two overlapping rectangles) until a popup appears, offering options such as "Close All". Select "Close All" to shut down every active tab instantly. * Periodically check and clear out unnecessary tabs throughout your browsing sessions to maintain optimal device functionality. Following these steps enables efficient tab management on your Samsung Galaxy S3, enhancing user experience while preserving resources like battery life and data allowance. Happy browsing! Title: How to Accept a Compliment Gracefully Introduction: Compliments are a form of appreciation and recognition that can boost your self-esteem and confidence. However, many people struggle with accepting compliments gracefully due to various reasons such as shyness, low self-confidence, or fear of being perceived as arrogant. This article will provide you with an in-depth guide on how to accept a compliment with grace and humility, helping you build positive relationships while also improving your own self-image. Step 1: Acknowledge the Compliment The first step in accepting a compliment is acknowledging its existence. When someone pays you a compliment, don't ignore it or brush it off casually. Instead, show gratitude by making eye contact, smiling, and saying thank you. Avoid dismissive responses like "it was nothing" or "anyone could have done it." These types of replies diminish both the giver's kind words and your worthiness of receiving them. Key tip: Make sure to express genuine appreciation; insincere thanks may come across as disingenuous. Step 2: Pause Before Responding Take a moment to process the compliment before responding. This pause shows respect for what has been said and gives you time to think about how you want to react. It also prevents you from rushing into a defensive or self-deprecating response. Guideline: Aim for a few seconds of silence after hearing a compliment. This small pause demonstrates thoughtfulness and mindfulness. Step 3: Show Appreciation Express your gratitude sincerely and concisely. You might say something like, "Thank you so much! I really appreciate your kind words," or "That means a lot to me, thank you." Remember, there is no need to downplay the compliment or make excuses - simply acknowledge it and move on. Key tip: Use specific language when thanking the person. For example, instead of saying "thank you," try "your praise means a great deal to me." Step 4: Return the Favor (Optional) If appropriate, return the favor by paying the person a compliment in return. Be sure that your compliment is heartfelt and relevant to the situation. This action reinforces positive interactions between you two and creates a supportive environment. Guideline: Keep your returned compliment balanced and related to the original one. For instance, if someone praises your cooking skills, you could respond with, "Thanks, I'm glad you enjoyed it. Your baking always impresses me too!" Step 5: Move On After accepting the compliment graciously, shift the conversation forward. Ask a question, share an interesting fact, or discuss another topic unrelated to the compliment. By doing this, you demonstrate confidence and ease in handling praise without becoming overly focused on yourself. Key tip: Steer clear of deflecting attention back onto yourself through self-deprecating humor or comments. Such actions undermine your growth in learning how to accept compliments gracefully. Conclusion: Learning how to accept a compliment with grace and humility takes practice but offers numerous benefits. Not only does it improve your self-esteem and relationships, but it also fosters a more positive outlook on life. So remember these steps next time someone pays you a kindness - acknowledge, pause, appreciate, possibly return the favor, then move on. We'll read it tonight before you go to bed. Image: Grey Nurse Shark tooth Illustration A tooth of a Grey Nurse Shark (Carcharias taurus). It is long and piercing with small cusps at the bottom. - Andrew Howells - Australian Museum - Common name: - Grey Nurse Shark - Scientific name: - Carcharias taurus Grey Nurse Sharks live near the sea floor and sometimes in the surf zone. They can be found down to depths of 190 m. Grey Nurse Sharks eat bony fishes, crabs, lobsters, squids and octopuses. They catch their food by rounding up small fishes into tight groups and eating them. Grey Nurse Sharks' teeth are long and piercing with small cusps. Their teeth constantly fall out and get replaced. Grey Nurse Sharks can grow up to 3.2 m long. Grey Nurse Sharks give birth to only two live young at a time. Grey Nurse Sharks are not dangerous, but divers should not annoy them. The Grey Nurse Shark has 'nurse' in its name because it rounds up fishes into a tight school. oh, maybe I did say that was the main reason. This wiki-style guide will provide you with detailed steps and useful tips to become an unforgettable first date, leaving your partner eager for a second encounter. Step 1: Plan A Unique Outing * Rationale: Show that you put thought into planning the date by choosing an activity or location that suits both of your interests and personalities. * Key Tips: + Avoid clich locations like coffee shops or movie theaters; instead, opt for less crowded places where conversation can flow more naturally. + Consider outdoor activities such as hiking, biking, or visiting local attractions. + If possible, incorporate something new and exciting for both of you, creating shared memories and strengthening your connection. Step 2: Dress Appropriately * Rationale: Clothing choices reflect your personality and attention to detail, making a significant impact on your date's perception of you. * Key Guidelines: + Dress comfortably yet stylishly, ensuring your attire matches the chosen venue. + Pay attention to grooming - shower, brush teeth, style hair, and wear deodorant. + Accessorize subtly to add flair without overdoing it. Step 3: Arrive Early and Greet Confidently * Rationale: Demonstrate punctuality and confidence from the start, easing any initial nervousness. * Key Steps: 1. Calculate travel time, including traffic and parking, to ensure timely arrival. 2. Upon meeting, make eye contact, smile warmly, and offer a firm handshake or hug (if appropriate). 3. Compliment your date sincerely but avoid generic comments about appearance. Step 4: Engage in Active Listening * Rationale: By actively listening during conversations, you show respect and genuine interest in getting to know your date better. * Key Techniques: 1. Focus completely on what they are saying, avoiding distractions. [2 sec silence, clatters of dishes, rustles] My bath must be ready by now. Essex is a county in the east of England. It has a population of around 1,750,000 people. It is part of the East of England region. Chelmsford is the county town of Essex, as well as being its only city. Essex has London and Hertfordshire on its western border and the whole of the east of the county is coast-line. The north of the county borders Suffolk and Cambridgeshire. The south of the county is on the Thames Estuary. The Dartford Tunnel can be used to drive under the River Thames between Essex and Kent. Title: How to Dress Your Small Children for Summer Introduction: Summer is a time for fun, sun, and outdoor activities. However, dressing small children for summer can be challenging due to their sensitive skin and higher risk of heatstroke. This guide will provide you with detailed steps to ensure your little ones stay cool, comfortable, and safe during the hot summer months. Step 1: Choose Lightweight Fabrics Lightweight fabrics like cotton, linen, or rayon are ideal for summer as they allow air circulation and absorb sweat better than synthetic materials. They also reduce the risk of rashes and heat-related discomforts. Key Tip: Look for loose fitting clothes made from breathable fabric to maximize comfort and ventilation. Step 2: Opt for Bright Colors Dark colors absorb more sunlight and heat, making them uncomfortable in hot weather. Instead, choose bright colored clothing that reflects sunlight and keeps your child cooler. Also, vibrant hues make your child easier to spot in crowded places! Guideline: Avoid black and other dark shades during summers. Step 3: Prioritize Sun Protection Sunburns aren't just painful; they increase the risk of skin cancer later in life. Therefore, always dress your child in UV-protective garments. Many brands now offer UPF (Ultraviolet Protection Factor) rated clothing which blocks most of the harmful UV rays. Key Tip: For added protection, consider sunglasses and wide-brimmed hats. Step 4: Consider Layering Even though it's summer, layering can still be beneficial. If you're going from an air-conditioned space into the sun, layers help adjust to temperature changes. Plus, if you're planning a picnic dinner, having a light cardigan handy can ward off evening chills. Just remember to keep all layers light and breathable. Step 5: Select Appropriate Footwear Opt for sandals or shoes made from natural materials like leather or canvas, which breathe well and prevent sweating. Make sure they fit properly to avoid blisters. Flip flops might seem convenient but they don't offer much support or protection against sharp objects. Suggestion: Try water shoes for beach trips - they protect feet from hot sand and rough surfaces while allowing easy draining when wet. Step 6: Don't Forget Swimwear & Beach Essentials For days spent by the pool or at the beach, pick quick-drying swimsuits to minimize discomfort caused by damp clothing. Rash guards provide extra sun protection too. Pack a microfiber towel as they're compact, lightweight, and super absorbent. Key Tip: Apply waterproof sunscreen regularly, especially after swimming. Step 7: Regularly Check Clothes Fit As children grow quickly, check frequently whether their clothes still fit correctly. Tight clothing can restrict movement and cause irritation, whereas oversized clothes may trip up active kids. Always ensure a good balance between comfort and freedom of movement. Conclusion: Proper attire plays a crucial role in ensuring your child enjoys the summer rather than enduring it. By following these steps, you can strike the perfect balance between style, comfort, and safety. Remember, happy children mean happier parents! One day, Jack was playing in the park. He was running around and having a great time. While Jack played, he saw something sparkly out of the corner of his eye. It was a toy tractor with a bright red shovel. Jack couldn't resist, so he slipped it into his pocket and kept playing. After he finished playing, Jack rushed home to show his parents his new toy. He showed them proudly and they applauded him. But then his father saw the toy and he said, "Jack, where did you get this?" Jack was very scared. He knew he should not have taken the toy. He started to cry and snivel. His father asked him again, "Did you take this from somewhere?" Jack sniffled and said, "Yes, it was in the park." His father was disappointed but he was understanding because Jack is only three years old. So his father said, "Okay Jack, we have to give it back to the park. Tomorrow morning you have to take it back and put it in the regular spot so it won't get lost again. Then you can come back to your crib and take a nap. Jack understood and nodded. So the next day, Jack and his father returned to the park. Jack placed the tractor in its original spot, and after that he was allowed to go back to his crib for a regular nap. From 1865 to 1870 the city was also the capital of the Kingdom of Italy. Florence is on the Arno River. The city has a population of around 400,000 people, and a suburban population of over 2,000,000 persons. The greater area has some 956,000 people. The city was a center of medieval European trade and finance. It is often considered the birthplace of the Italian Renaissance and was long ruled by the Medici family. Florence is also famous for its fine art and architecture. It is said that, of the 1,000 most important European artists of the second millennium, 350 lived or worked in Florence. Today, we're going to have some fun with arrays of numbers and explore a cool puzzle involving their "determinants." Don't worry if you haven't heard about determinants before; just think of them as special values associated with square arrays of numbers, like 2x2 or 3x3 grids. Let's start with a smaller example using a 2x2 array, or grid: ```css [ a , b ] [ c , d ] ``` The determinant of this grid is calculated by multiplying the top left number `a` with bottom right number `d`, then subtracting the product of top right number `b` with bottom left number `c`. So its determinant would be `ad - bc`. Now, imagine having larger grids, say 3x3, filled with numbers. The process remains similar, but calculating becomes more complex due to additional numbers involved. However, our focus today isn't on finding these determinants, but rather understanding why certain properties may hold or not hold between different grids. Where did you improve, and where do you need some extra practice? For example, if you got question 15 wrong on the pretest, did you get it right on the posttest? The binomial 25x16 - 144 is the difference between two perfect squares. The coefficient of x, 25, is a perfect square, and the exponent of x is even. The constant, 144, is also a perfect square. The square root of 25x16 is 5x8, and the square root of 144 is 12. The square root of the first term, 5x8, plus the square root of the second term, 12, is the first factor: 5x8 + 12. The square root of the first term minus the square root of the second term is the second factor: 5x8 - 12. Therefore, 25x16 - 144 factors to (5x8 + 12)(5x8 - 12). d. The equation x2 + 7x + 5 = 0 is in the form ax2 + bx + c = 0, but this trinomial cannot be factored easily because its roots are not integers. Use the quadratic formula. The coefficient of x2 is 1, so a = 1. The coefficient of x is 7, so b = 7, and the constant is 5, so c = 5. Substitute these values into the quadratic formula: Next, (7)2 = 49 and (4)(1)(5) = 20: Under the square root symbol, 49 - 20 = 29, and in the denominator, 2(1) = 2: The square root of 29 cannot be simplified. One root is equal to , and the other root is equal to . d. We're looking for the original size of Nancy's farm, so we can use x to represent that number. Sherry buys one-eighth of Nancy's farm, which means that she buys acres. Sherry already had 6 acres, which means that the size of her farm is now . We know that her farm is 10 acres, so we can set equal to 10 and solve for x: x = 32 Nancy's farm was 32 acres. b. The number of students who wear glasses is unknown, so represent that number with x. The number of students who wear contact lenses is 36 less than four times the number of students who wear glasses, which means that the number of students who wear contact lenses is equal to 4x - 36. The ratio of students who wear contact lenses to students who wear glasses is 7:2, or . The ratio of actual students who wear contact lenses to actual students who wear glasses is (4x - 36): x, or . These ratios are equal. Write a proportion and solve for . Multiply both sides by 2x: 7x = 8x - 72. Subtract 8x from both sides: -x = -72, so x = 72. Therefore, 72 students wear glasses. a. The probability of selecting a can of mushroom soup is equal to the number of cans of mushroom soup divided by the total number of cans of soup. Let x represent the number of cans of mushroom soup Kara buys. There are now 4 + x cans of mushroom soup, and 4 + x + 10 + 8 = 22 + x total cans. The probability of selecting a can of mushroom soup is equal to . To find the value of x that will make this probability equal to , set the two fractions equal to each other, cross multiply, and solve for x: 4(4 + x) = 1(22 + x) 16 + 4x = 22 + x 16 + 3x = 22 3x = 6 x = 2 For the probability of selecting a can of mushroom soup to become , Kara must buy 2 cans of mushroom soup. d. The original value is unknown, so let x represent the original value. The new value is 93. Percent decrease is equal to the original value minus the new value, all divided by the original value. Subtract 93 from x and divide by x. Set that fraction equal to 22.5%, which is 0.225: Multiply both sides of the equation by x, and then subtract x from both sides: 0.225x = x - 93 -0.775x = -93 Divide by -0.775: x = 120. Therefore, 120, after a 22.5% decrease, is 93. c. The formula tn = t1(rn - 1) gives us the value of any term in a geometric sequence. The first term, t1, is x - 8. The ratio, r, can be found by dividing any term by the previous term. Divide the fourth term by the third term, because this will cause the variable x to be canceled: 9x -3x = -3, so r = -3. Because we are looking for the sixth term, n = 6: t6 = (x - 8)(-36 - 1) t6 = (x - 8)(-35) t6 = (x - 8)(-243) t6 = -243x + 1,944 The ratio of any term in the sequence to the previous term is -3. That means that the second term, 2x - 6, divided by the first term, x - 8, is equal to -3. The formula for area of a triangle is . Let x represent the base of the triangle. Because the height is two less than four times the base, the height of the triangle is 4x - 2. Substitute x for b and 4x - 2 for h. Substitute 45 for A: 45 = 2x2 - x 2x2 - x - 45 = 0 Factor this quadratic equation and find the solutions for x. The factors of 2x2 are -2, -1, 1, 2, -x, and x. The first terms of the binomials may be 2x and x. The factors of -45 are -45, -15, -9, -5, -3, -1, 1, 3, 5, 9, 15, and 45. We need two factors that multiply to -45, which means that we need one positive number and one negative number: (2x - 5)(x + 9) = 2x2 + 13x - 45. The middle term is too large. (2x - 9)(x + 5) = 2x2 + x - 45. The middle term is the wrong sign. (2x + 9)(x - 5) = 2x2 - x - 45. Date: 04/16/97 at 14:24:23 From: JARED ROSS Subject: Algebra If two consecutive numbers are less than one hundred, what is the larger number? Date: 06/25/97 at 17:10:03 From: Doctor Sydney Subject: Re: Algebra Dear Jared, Hello! Thanks for writing. In reading your question, we came up with a couple of different ways to interpret it, so we aren't quite sure what you mean. One way we might intepret the question is the following: 1. Suppose we have two consecutive numbers, each of which is less than 100. What is the larger number? Or, you might mean: 2. Suppose that we have two consecutive numbers such that their sum is less than 100. Then what is the larger number? This second question is more difficult than the first, so we'll assume that that is what you mean. However, if you were really asking the first question and still need help with it, write back, and we can help out. So, let's look at the second interpretation. Well, there are many pairs of numbers that when added up, equal less than 100. Think of it this way: If the two numbers must add up to be less than 100 (and we are only dealing with integers, which are numbers of the type ...,-3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3,...), then we can express what we want as: n + (n + 1) < 100 This says that n, plus the number that comes right after n, must be equal to a number less than 100. But we can make this even simpler! The equation can also be written as: 2n + 1 < 100 2n < 99 n < 49.5 So, for any pair of consecutive numbers (n, n+1) such that n < 49.5, the sum of the consecutive numbers is less than 100. For instance, the consecutive numbers (49,50) add up to less than 100. In fact 49 and 50 are the largest consecutive numbers that have this property. See if you can figure out why. Can you find other examples of consecutive numbers that work? I hope this helps you. Please write back if we answered the wrong question or if you need more help. -Doctors Matthew and Sydney, The Math Forum Check out our web site! http://mathforum.org/dr.math/ Search the Dr. Math Library: Ask Dr. MathTM 1994-2013 The Math Forum I wondered what he was talking about. "Monosyllables" are words that have only one vowel sound; "polysyllables" have more than one. If a syllable ends with a consonant, it is called a "closed syllable". If a syllable ends with a vowel, it is called an "open syllable". Patterns of syllables can be shown with C and V (C for 'consonant', V for 'vowel'). Closed syllables are often shown as "CVC" (such as "got"), and open syllables as "CV" (such as "go"). Some languages like English have many kinds of closed syllables. Other languages, like Japanese, have few kinds of closed syllables. Other languages still, like Hawaiian and Swahili, have no closed syllables. What do you want me to say? A circle (drawn or printed) 2. Ruler or straight edge 3. Protractor 4. Pencil 5. Compass (optional) Step 1: Drawing Your Circle First, you need to have a circle that you want to divide into six equal parts. If you don't already have one, draw a circle using a compass or any round object as guide, making sure it is large enough for easy manipulation but small enough to fit within your workspace. Key Tips & Guidelines: - The size of the circle doesn't matter when dividing it into equal sections; however, larger circles may require more precision due to increased space between divisions. - Using a compass ensures an accurately drawn circle with consistent radius throughout. Step 2: Marking Points Along the Circumference With your ruler or straight edge, mark two points along the circumference of the circle - these will serve as starting points for our division lines. They should be spaced evenly around the circle but not directly opposite each other. Ideally, they should form a smaller angle less than 90 degrees at the center of the circle. Key Tips & Guidelines: - For accuracy, measure the distance from the center of the circle to the first point, then duplicate this measurement when locating the second point. This guarantees both points are equidistant from the center. Step 3: Measuring Angles with a Protractor Place the pointed end of your protractor at the center of the circle, aligning its base line with one of the radial lines passing through either marked point. Read off the degree value where the other radial line intersects the protractor scale. Step 4: Calculating Required Degrees Since we aim to divide the circle into six equal parts, each section must subtend an angle of 360 6 = 60 at the center. Therefore, calculate the difference between the currently measured angle and 60 . Step 5: Creating Additional Radial Lines Subtract or add the calculated difference alternately to the previous angles while drawing new radial lines. Continue doing so until five additional lines have been created, ensuring all resulting angles are close to 60 . Key Tips & Guidelines: - Be careful about adding or subtracting values; addition moves clockwise whereas subtraction goes counterclockwise. - It might be challenging to create perfect 60 angles without prior knowledge of geometry or trigonometry. However, getting them as close as possible would suffice for most applications requiring divided circles. Step 6: Connecting Intersections Draw line segments connecting the intersection points formed by adjacent radial lines across the circle. These lines represent the boundaries separating the six equally sized sectors. Congratulations! You now know how to divide a circle into six equal parts using basic tools and simple steps. Remember, precision isn't always necessary depending on what you plan to do with the divided circle. But if high accuracy is required, consider studying advanced techniques involving geometric constructions or digital software solutions. Do you like to go again go fishing again? Title: How to Clean Whiskey Stones Whiskey stones are small cubes made of soapstone or another non-porous material that can be chilled and used to cool whiskey without diluting it like ice does. Over time, these stones may accumulate dirt, odors, and residue from repeated use. To ensure your whiskey stones continue to enhance the flavor of your favorite spirits, it's important to clean them regularly. Here is an in-depth guide on cleaning whiskey stones effectively and safely. **Things You'll Need:** * Dish soap * Soft bristle brush (such as a toothbrush) * Distilled water * Airtight container * Paper towel or soft cloth **Step 1: Gather Your Supplies** Before starting the cleaning process, make sure you have all necessary supplies within reach. This will help maintain organization throughout the task and prevent any potential cross-contamination with other kitchen items. **Step 2: Rinse Off Loose Debris** Rinse the whiskey stones under warm running water to remove any loose debris or particles. Be gentle during this step; avoid using high pressure which could push residue further into crevices. **Step 3: Create Soapy Solution** Fill a bowl with warm water and add several drops of dish soap. Mix until suds form. The warmth of the water opens up the stone's pores making it easier to lift out trapped impurities. Dish soap is designed to cut through grease and grime, ensuring thorough cleaning. **Step 4: Scrub Delicately** Using a soft bristle brush, gently scrub each whiskey stone one at a time. Pay special attention to areas where residue might build up such as corners and edges. Remember, whiskey stones are softer than most common materials, so they scratch easily. Using too much force could damage their surface. **Key Tip**: If stains persist after initial scrubbing, let the stones sit submerged in the soapy solution for about 15 minutes before attempting to scrub again. **Step 5: Rinse Thoroughly** After scrubbing, rinse the whiskey stones individually under warm running water. Make sure no soap remains as leftover residues can affect taste. Check visually for remaining dirt or food particles and repeat steps if needed. **Step 6: Dry Completely** Place the cleaned whiskey stones on a paper towel or soft cloth. Allow them to air dry completely before storage. Moisture left behind can lead to bacterial growth and unpleasant smells. It also prevents proper freezing later. **Step 7: Store Properly** Once dried, store the whiskey stones in an airtight container. Opaque containers are best because light exposure can cause discoloration over time. Also, keeping them sealed keeps away dust, moisture, and unwanted smells. **Guidelines For Regular Maintenance:** * Always wash your hands before handling whiskey stones. * Never put whiskey stones in the dishwasher or expose them to extreme temperatures. * Sanitize your whisky stones once a month by boiling them in distilled water for five minutes followed by immediate cooling and drying. By following these simple yet meticulous steps, you're ensured sparkling clean whiskey stones ready for every occasion! Enjoy your perfectly chilled drinks knowing full well that your whiskey stones are hygienically maintained. And I think the pink one is strawberry. Observing the Pumpkin Cycle - Grades: PreK-K - Unit Plan: About this book - Learn about the life cycle of a pumpkin. - Observe growth and decay. - Learn where food comes from. - Practice early reading skills through a shared reading. - Pumpkin Circle: The Story of a Garden by George Levenson - Chart paper for recording student observations during the read-aloud - Pots, soil, - Pumpkin seeds - Piece of a pumpkin - Student Observation Journals (PDF) - Five Little Pumpkins by Iris Van Rynback - Pocket Chart for Shared Reading - Sentence Strips Set Up and Prepare - Create student journals for their observations. - Cut up a pumpkin. - Make sentence strips with the text from Five Little Pumpkins and mount in the pocket chart for the shared reading. Part I: Nonfiction Reading This unit is a great opportunity to bring nonfiction literature to the read-aloud time and introduce your students to facts about the world around them. This lesson is just one of many great books that I like to use to teach the life cycle and plant growth. I follow up this reading with many other books to grow students' understanding. Step 1: Before reading, ask students questions about where pumpkins (and other foods) come from. Then introduce the book and let the students know that it's going to help us learn about where pumpkins come from and how they grow. Step 2: Read Pumpkin Circle: The Story of a Garden. Stop to discuss what they notice and learn about pumpkin growth. Chart the sequence of the pumpkins as students recount the story. Step 3: Review with the students what they've learned about growing pumpkins and tell them they'll get to grow a pumpkin plant in class. Give each student a pot, some potting soil, and several seeds to plant. Show the students how to dig a hole in the soil and plant their seeds. Guide the students when watering their seeds. You'll need to water and put the pots in the sun daily. You may want to start this part after the seeds are just about to sprout. Step 4: Review with students what they learned about what happens to pumpkins when they get old. Discuss with them what they do with old fruits and vegetables at their house. Ask them: Have they ever seen fruit that is starting to decay, or get old and yucky? What do they think happens to that fruit? What did they learn in Pumpkin Circle about pumpkins that get old and mushy? (Introduce the words decay and decompose.) Step 5: Tell the students that we are going to be watching the beginning and end of the pumpkin cycle. Talk with them about how we're going to be able to watch a pumpkin grow. Then cut a piece of a pumpkin and show them that we're going to keep it in a jar (so it won't smell) and watch the pumpkin grow old. Step 6: Pass out the life cycle journals. Tell students they are going to record (with pictures or words) what they see as they take care of their seeds and watch the pumpkin decompose. Look for ways to connect the students' observations to books you are reading and things they are learning. Students will take their plants home after they have grown to a predetermined height. Part IV: Shared Reading The story Five Little Pumpkins is a great book to use as a shared reading with this unit. Shared readings are a non-threatening environment for early readers to practice tracking, fluency, phrasing, and sight words. As the students become familiar with the text, I use it for skill-based mini-lessons. Step 7: Read the poem from the sentence strips you had already prepared. While reading, point to the words with a long pointer. Once the students are comfortable with the poem, let volunteers lead the reading with the pointer. Optional: Have the students make pumpkin puppets and act out the poem as we read it together. Cover the 2nd rhyming word of each two line rhyming pair. Ask students if they know the word that is covered. Ask if they can think of another word that will rhyme with the uncovered word. Five Little Pumpkins, sitting on a gate The first one said, "Oh my it's getting (the word late is covered) Ask students if they can think of other words that rhyme with the words "gate, air, care, light." Record their responses on a chart and keep the chart up and they can add to the list as throughout the week. Supporting All Learners Encourage students who don't normally raise their hand during discussions. Give them the opportunity to read the poem to you or the class -- as long as they feel comfortable doing so. Allow sufficient wait time for students to gather their thoughts and formulate their answers. Some students will be encouraged to read the pocket charts when the pointers are a different shape, size, or perhaps something fun like a large hand. Take students to see a real pumpkin patch with a field trip. Have parents talk with their children about the food they buy at the grocery store. Where does that food come from? How does it grow? Perhaps they can find smaller and larger pumpkins at the store. Students will create pumpkin puppets and paint pumpkins. They will participate in a shared reading of a poem, and discuss the stages of a pumpkin's growth. - Are the students incorporating things learned from the read-aloud into their thinking? - Were they interested in the topic? - Did they go to the pocket charts on their own time and read them? - Do you see the reading skills practiced during shared reading when students are reading independently? - Did students participate in discussions? - Do students participate in the shared reading? In humans a fetus is the stage (time of development) after the embryonic stage, and begins during the third to eighth week of development after fertilization of the egg. Embedding HD videos in Weebly without upgrading to their pro plan is an excellent way to enhance your website's content while keeping costs down. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step with clear explanations and helpful tips along the way. **Step 1: Choose Your Video Hosting Platform** There are several free video hosting platforms that allow embedding HD videos, such as YouTube, Vimeo, or Dailymotion. These websites have built-in players which handle different aspects like adaptive bitrate streaming (ABS), providing optimal video quality based on users' internet speeds. For this example, we will be using YouTube since it has wide acceptance and seamless integration with Weebly. **Step 2: Upload Your Video To The Chosen Platform** Once you have chosen your platform, create an account if necessary and then upload your HD video file. Make sure the resolution is set at 720p or higher for true high definition. After uploading, give the video some time to finish processing before proceeding to the next steps. **Step 3: Get The Video Embed Code** After processing, locate the 'Share', 'Embed', or similar button under your video. Clicking this option should provide you with an embed code snippet - usually HTML containing an iframe tag. Copy this entire block of code; it contains important information about the video source and player settings. **Step 4: Access The Weebly Site Editor** Log into your Weebly account, navigate to the site where you want to add the embedded video, and click on 'Edit'. You'll now see the familiar drag-and-drop editor interface. **Step 5: Add An Embed Code Element** Locate the left sidebar menu, find the 'Elements' tab, scroll down until you find 'Embed Code', and drag it onto the page layout where you wish the video to appear. Release the mouse button when you see a dashed rectangle indicating drop location. **Step 6: Paste The Copied Embed Code** Click inside the newly added empty embed box. A small window labelled 'Custom HTML' appears. Delete any existing text within it and paste the copied YouTube embed code here instead. Remember, all highlighted code from earlier needs to go here. **Step 7: Adjust Settings If Necessary** While most default settings work fine, you can adjust parameters like size or autoplay by modifying the attributes within the iframe tags. However, note that changing these might affect responsiveness or user experience so tread carefully. **Step 8: Save Changes & Publish** Save your changes by clicking on the green checkmark icon at the top right corner of the screen. Preview your site to ensure everything looks good. Once satisfied, hit the blue 'Publish' button located beside the save button. Congratulations! You've successfully embedded an HD video into your Weebly site without needing a pro plan. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Always respect copyright laws when choosing videos to embed. * Use thumbnails wisely to make your page look more attractive. * Keep the aspect ratio consistent between the hosted video and the embedded space for best display results. * Test your embedded video across various devices and browsers to ensure compatibility. * Consider adding captions or subtitles for accessibility purposes. Mutations are when a sound (in speech) or a letter (in writing) changes at the start of a word. An example is the Welsh word ""gwneud"", which in English means "to do", and ""dod"", which means "to come" "dewch i mewn" which means "come in". Sometimes the word changes from ""gwneud"" to ""wneud"", and from ""dod"" to ""ddod"". These sounds (in speech) or letters (in writing) changes also occur within, and at the end, of words, although the simplified classification found in ordinary books does not mention this. Approximately three-fourths of people with schizophrenia have an ongoing disability with symptoms that keep coming back. Some people do recover completely and others function well in society. Most people with schizophrenia live independently, with community support. In people with a first episode of serious mental symptoms, 42 percent have a good long-term outcome, 35 percent have an intermediate outcome and 27 percent have a poor outcome. Outcomes for schizophrenia appear better in the developing world than in the developed world, although that conclusion has been questioned. Title: How to Be a Detective for Kids Introduction: Welcome to this comprehensive guide on becoming a detective for kids! This tutorial will provide you with all the necessary steps, tips, and guidelines to help you solve mysteries like a pro. Remember, being a detective requires patience, attention to detail, and critical thinking skills. So grab your notebook and let's get started! Step 1: Understand What It Means to be a Detective A detective is someone who gathers evidence and solves crimes or mysteries using logical reasoning and problem-solving abilities. As a kid detective, focus on solving smaller-scale mysteries such as lost items, hidden objects, or uncovering secrets within your family or friend group. Key Tip: Always remember that real detectives have strict rules they must follow, including respecting people's privacy and staying safe. Follow these same principles while practicing your detective skills. Step 2: Develop Observation Skills Observation is crucial when trying to solve any mystery. Train yourself to notice things other people might miss by paying close attention to details around you. Look for clues like unusual patterns, missing items, or odd behaviors. Key Guideline: Practice observing different environments, such as parks, schools, and homes, so you can become familiar with what's normal and spot abnormalities more easily. Step 3: Improve Your Listening Abilities Listening carefully to others can provide valuable information during an investigation. Pay attention to tone, body language, and specific words used by those involved in the case. Key Tip: When talking to potential witnesses, ask open-ended questions rather than yes/no questions to encourage them to share more information. Step 4: Learn Basic Research Techniques Conduct background research on people, places, and events related to your case. Use resources like books, interviews, and online databases (with parental guidance) to gather relevant information. Key Guideline: Keep track of where you found each piece of information for future reference. Step 5: Organize Collected Data Create a system for organizing the data you collect during your investigations. You can use charts, diagrams, or even create a digital spreadsheet to keep everything neatly categorized and easy to access. Key Tip: Regularly update your records as new information becomes available. Step 6: Analyze Evidence Critically Examine all collected evidence objectively and look for connections between seemingly unrelated pieces of information. Consider alternative explanations and test hypotheses before drawing conclusions. Key Guideline: Avoid jumping to conclusions based on assumptions; instead, rely on solid evidence to support your theories. Step 7: Communicate Findings Effectively Present your findings clearly and logically, making sure to explain how you arrived at your conclusion. Use visual aids if possible, and practice speaking confidently about your work. Key Tip: Encourage feedback from others and be prepared to answer questions about your methods and discoveries. Step 8: Stay Safe While Investigating Always prioritize safety when conducting investigations. Never put yourself in danger or invade someone else's privacy. If you feel uncomfortable or unsafe, stop immediately and seek adult assistance. Key Guideline: Always involve a trusted adult when dealing with potentially risky situations or sensitive topics. Conclusion: By following these steps and honing your observation, listening, research, organization, analysis, communication, and safety skills, you'll be well on your way to becoming a skilled young detective. Happy sleuthing! Rabbi can't build anythin' he doesn't know how. When I was at school somebody there kicked somebody out of the swing. Did you just get yourself some more paper? She came running into the room and said, "Lucy, what are you doing? You should keep the flute away and leave it alone". But Lucy wasn't ready to stop playing with the flute. She kept blowing on it and was having too much fun! In the end, mom had to take the flute away from her little girl and promised her that if she listened to her mom, she'd give her a new one. In the 1980s, Dieter Seitzer started the development of a code for audio format of high-quality. Together with his team at the "German Fraunhofer Institut" situated in Erlangen, the professor had the idea to create code with a low bit-rate. The German institute received the patent for MP3 in 1989. MP3 was then passed to the International Organisation for Standardisation. In the same year the MP3 was included into MPEG-1 specification. [shows Maisie that it is the end of the book] How to Get Up from a Fall - If you fall, remain still on the floor or ground for a few moments. - Decide if you are hurt. Getting up too quickly or the wrong way could make an injury worse. - If you think you can get up safely, roll over onto your side. - Rest a bit to let your body and blood pressure adjust. - Slowly get up on your hands and knees and crawl to a sturdy chair. - Place your hands on the seat of the chair and slide one foot forward so that it is flat on the floor. Keep the other leg bent with the knee on the floor. - From this kneeling position, slowly rise and turn your body to sit in the chair. Title: How to Make a Hair Bow Making a hair bow is an easy and fun way to add some flair to your hairstyle or accessorize a gift. With just a few simple materials and steps, you can create a stylish hair bow that will turn heads. This tutorial provides a comprehensive guide on creating a classic hair bow using ribbon. **Materials Needed:** 1. Ribbon (at least 5/8 inch wide) - Choose a color and pattern that suits your personal style. A length of about 24 inches should be sufficient for making one standard-sized bow. 2. Alligator clip or bobby pins - These will help secure the bow onto your hair. Select based on your preference and the thickness of your hair. 3. Scissors - Sharp scissors are essential for clean cuts and preventing fraying. 4. Hot glue gun and hot glue sticks (optional) - If you want a more permanent hold, you may opt to use a hot glue gun instead of relying solely on the alligator clip or bobby pins. Be cautious when handling as the glue can get extremely hot. **Instructions:** Step 1: Cut the Ribbon Cut two pieces of ribbon approximately 12 inches each. These will form the loops of the bow. Additionally, cut another piece of ribbon around 3-4 inches; this smaller strip will serve as the centerpiece or 'knot' of the bow. Key Tip: Using scissors with sharp points ensures precise cuts and minimizes frayed edges. Step 2: Create Loops Take one of the longer strips of ribbon and fold it in half, forming a loop. Hold both ends together and press them flat against a tabletop so they align evenly. Repeat this process with the second long strip. Guideline: The size of the loops depends on how large you want your finished bow to be. Adjust accordingly but keep in mind that bigger loops might require stronger clips or additional support from bobby pins. Step 3: Attach Centerpiece Place the three loops side by side, ensuring their bases are aligned. Now take the shorter strip of ribbon and wrap it over the base of the loops where they meet, covering any rough edges. Apply a small amount of hot glue if necessary to keep it in place. However, avoid applying too much glue as it could seep through and burn your fingers. Alternative Method without Glue: Instead of using glue, simply twist the short end of the third piece tightly around the base several times before securing it with either an alligator clip or bobby pin(s). Step 4: Form Knot Once secured, flip the entire arrangement upside down. Take the loose end of the wrapped ribbon and pull it upward, creating a twisted effect similar to tying shoelaces. Continue pulling until the twisted section becomes tight enough to hold its shape. Then, bring the end back down and tuck it under the twists. Step 5: Secure Bow Using either an alligator clip or bobbies pins, attach the newly formed bow to your preferred location on your head. For thinner hair types, multiple bobby pins strategically placed can provide extra stability. Final Key Tips: - Always test the security of your bow after attaching it to ensure it won't fall off during wear. - Practice makes perfect! Don't worry if your first attempt isn't perfect. Keep trying and soon you'll master the art of making beautiful hair bows. And Santa Claus brought you the big dog too, didn't he? My name is Kana what's your name? VB 1.0 was introduced in 1991. The drag and drop design for creating the user interface is derived from a prototype form generator developed by Alan Cooper and his company called "Tripod". Microsoft contracted with Cooper and his associates to develop Tripod into a programmable form system for Windows 3.0, under the code name "Ruby" (no relation to the Ruby programming language). Tripod did not include a programming language at all. Microsoft decided to combine Ruby with the Basic language to create Visual Basic. The Ruby interface generator provided the "visual" part of Visual Basic and this was combined with the "EB" Embedded BASIC engine designed for Microsoft's abandoned "Omega" database system. Ruby also provided the ability to load dynamic link libraries containing additional controls (then called "gizmos"), which later became the Visual Basic Extension (VBX) interface. In baseball, a hit is when a player (called the batter or batsman) who is trying to hit the ball that is thrown (or pitched) by the thrower (called the pitcher) hits the ball in such a way as to let him move forward at least one base. How a hit is made [change] The pitcher throws the ball towards the batter standing at home plate (or home base), who tries to hit the ball and score a run. After the batter hits the ball he runs as fast as he can around the bases and tries to get to a base before he is called out by an umpire. If he gets to a base before he is called out he is safe and the next batter comes forward and tries to hit a ball from the pitcher. The old batter who is on base becomes a runner. Types of hit [change] - single - a hit that moves the batter one base towards home plate. - double - a hit that moves the batter two bases towards home plate. - triple - a hit that moves the batter three bases towards home plate. - home run - a hit that moves the batter all four bases and back to home plate. There are other kinds of hits, but these are the major ones. These hits can be made by many ways. For example; a single can come from a hit that travelled along the ground (called a grounder) or that travelled in the air (a fly ball). A grounder can often be harder for the other team to stop than a fly ball. This is because when it moves along the ground it has more chance of hitting bumps and the glove or foot of the other teams player than it does if it moves in the air. There are also more ways in which hits can happen but these are the two major ways. A no-hitter [change] A no-hitter is a form of game in which the other team does not even get a single hit during the whole game. Because of the skill of the players a no-hitter does not happen very often. What does an umbrella do for you when it's hot? Hey Mommy now we going to be rich, aren't we? The largest gaps in the rings are the Cassini Division and the Encke Division, both visible from the Earth. The Cassini Division is the largest, measuring wide. However, when the Voyager spacecrafts visited Saturn in 1980, they discovered that the rings are a complex structure, made out of thousands of thin gaps and ringlets. Scientists believe this is caused by the gravitational force of some of Saturn's moons. The tiny moon Pan orbits inside Saturn's rings, creating a gap within the rings. Other ringlets keep their structure due to the gravitational force of shepherd satellites, such as Prometheus and Pandora. Other gaps form due to the gravitational force of a large moon farther away. The moon Mimas is responsible for clearing away the Cassini gap. [regarding MOT's position while sitting on the floor] XXX LE' me get my shoes on. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Parent Child/SLI/PDF/deb24.cha = = = [Nina continues yelling and bouncing the dog around.] "Where in the world is......" AUTHOR: Jean Ann Hollar, Washington I have students from the third and fourth grade. They have a heck of a time figuring out where any of the places on the map are. Also, they ask if Canada is one of the states. They think that New Mexico is part of the country Mexico. For these reasons, we play a game called "Where in the world is..." every morning. GRADE LEVEL/SUBJECT: This game can be adjusted to any grade level. I'll give the directions for playing with my third and fourth graders. PURPOSE: I want my students to know if I'm talking about a country or a state in our country. They should see that there is a World, that World has continents, those continents have countries within them, and those countries are divided up into smaller sections, such as states. OBJECTIVES: The students will be able to transfer what they see on the globe to a flat map. They will be familiar with large or major land masses as well as major rivers. The students will be able to point out major countries of the world as well as major cities. RESOURCES/MATERIALS: One large wall map of the world Several globes Tag board and yarn and pins or tape ACTIVITIES AND PROCEDURES: Have a large wall map of the world on a bulletin board with space around it. Also, have a globe of the world. Cut out large letters spelling, "Where in the world is....?" and put this above the wall map. Have two spaces available to the right of the wall map. These will hold tag board pieces cut to the same size. They will have different names on them each day. Attach a piece of yarn to the tag board. It will be attached to a different spot on the map each day. The second space for tag board is for later on when city names are added to the question. This also requires a piece of yarn. On the first day, allow the students to become familiar with the similarities between the wall map and the globe. The first day tag board should read "the world". Days Two through Eight: Have the name of one of the continents up on the first tag board space when the children enter in the morning. Allow them to use the globe to find the answer to the question, "Where in the world is....Africa?" When the children have had a chance to decide, let one come up and attach the yarn to the correct area of the map that answers the question of that day. Days Nine through (?): Take this time to point out major oceans and seas. Each day ask what the students know about the area that is high- lighted. Day (?) When you are ready to begin asking where a country is, begin using the second tag board section for a city in that country. TYING IT ALL TOGETHER: I use the first part of the school year to practice mapping skills. I have the students map out the classroom, the school, and a fantasy island. As they practice finding real places on the wall map and globe, it helps them see the relationship between the paper and the real world. Thanks, Jean Ann for such a great lesson plan. I update the site often, so your great work could be showcased really soon. Thanks for the assistance and thanks for the great work! no, the chimney goes on this side. Getting Your Child to Use a Baby Walker: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: A baby walker is a device designed to help babies learn to walk by supporting them as they take their first steps. While there are differing opinions about the safety and benefits of using baby walkers, many parents find that they can be helpful tools when used correctly and under supervision. This guide will provide you with clear and practical instructions on how to encourage your child to use a baby walker safely and effectively. Step 1: Choose an Appropriate Baby Walker Before attempting to get your child to use a baby walker, it's essential to select one that meets current safety standards and is appropriate for your child's age, weight, and developmental stage. Look for walkers with wide bases and non-slip feet to prevent tipping over or sliding on smooth surfaces. Additionally, ensure that any toys or activities included on the walker are safe and engaging for your child. Step 2: Familiarize Your Child with the Baby Walker Place your child in the baby walker while seated and securely fastened according to manufacturer instructions. Allow your child to explore the walker at their own pace, becoming familiar with its features and learning how to operate the various buttons, levers, or toys attached to it. Be sure to remain close by during this process to monitor your child's safety and offer assistance if needed. Key Tips: * Always adhere to the recommended age range provided by the manufacturer. * Double-check all straps and buckles to make sure they are properly secured before use. Step 3: Encourage Movement Once your child seems comfortable sitting in the baby walker, gently hold onto the frame and slowly move it back and forth to demonstrate motion. As your child begins to understand how the walker moves, gradually reduce your support until they can propel themselves forward independently. Offering verbal encouragement throughout this process can also help build confidence and excitement around walking. Key Guidelines: * Never leave your child unattended in the baby walker. * Only allow use on flat, even surfaces free from obstacles or hazards. Step 4: Monitor Progress and Adjust Accordingly As your child becomes more proficient in using the baby walker, adjust the height setting accordingly to maintain proper posture and promote correct leg alignment. Continue monitoring your child's progress and transition to other forms of mobility aid (such as push toys) once they have outgrown the need for a baby walker. Additional Safety Considerations: * Keep doors closed and gates latched to prevent access to stairs or other potentially dangerous areas. * Remove any tripping hazards such as loose rugs or electrical cords from the room where the baby walker is being used. * Regularly inspect the baby walker for wear and tear, ensuring all components are secure and functioning correctly. Conclusion: While getting your child to use a baby walker may require patience and persistence, following these steps carefully can create a positive experience that supports their early mobility milestones. By prioritizing safety, closely supervising usage, and making gradual adjustments based on your child's needs, you can help foster independence and confidence in their ability to explore the world around them. Many birds are not camouflaged, but stand out with vivid colours. They are usually male birds whose females are dull and camouflaged. The function of the colours is two-fold. First, the colours help them get mates, and second, the colours identify them to other males of the same species. Many birds are territorial, especially in the nesting season. They give out territory sounds and are easily seen. This lets other males know they will defend their territory. Title: How to Download Videos on Periscope on Android Periscope is a popular live streaming platform that allows users to broadcast and view videos from all over the world. While the app does not have a built-in feature to download videos, there are third-party tools available that can help you save your favorite Periscope streams directly onto your Android device. This tutorial will guide you through the process of downloading Periscope videos using an Android smartphone or tablet. **Disclaimer:** Using third-party tools may infringe upon copyright laws and Periscope's terms of service. Always ensure that you have permission from the content creator before downloading their video. ### Step 1: Choose a Third-Party Tool There are several third-party tools available online that allow you to download Periscope videos on Android devices. Some popular options include: * **TechJunkie**: A website offering a simple method to download Periscope videos by copying and pasting the URL into their search bar. (<https://www.techjunkie.com/download-periscope-videos/>) * **SaveFromNet**: Another website where you can paste the Periscope video link and download the file as an MP4 format. (<https://en.savefrom.net/#> ) * **Periscope Downloader App**: An application specifically designed for downloading Periscope videos on Android devices. You can find this app in the Google Play Store. For the purpose of this tutorial, we will be demonstrating with TechJunkie. However, the steps should be similar across different platforms. ### Step 2: Find the Periscope Video To download a video, first locate the Periscope stream you want to save. Open the Periscope app on your Android device and navigate to the desired video. Once you find it, click on the video and wait for it to load completely. ### Step 3: Copy the Video Link With the video fully loaded, tap the share button usually located at the bottom right corner of the screen. From the list of sharing options, select 'Copy link.' Title: How to Fix a Squeaking Ceiling Fan Introduction: A squeaking ceiling fan can be an annoying problem, but fortunately, it's usually easy to fix with just a few tools and some household items. This guide will walk you through the process step-by-step so that you can have your fan running smoothly again in no time. Things You'll Need: * Ladder or stool * Screwdriver (Phillips or flathead depending on your fan) * Light lubricant such as WD-40 or silicone spray * Clean cloth or paper towel * Optional: Gloves and safety glasses Step 1: Turn Off Power Before starting any repair work on your ceiling fan, make sure power is turned off at both the switch and circuit breaker to prevent accidents while working near electrical components. Step 2: Access the Motor Housing Using a ladder or stool, carefully climb up and locate the screws holding the fan blades to the motor housing. These are typically found on the top side of the blade arm where it meets the motor assembly. Unscrew these completely and set them aside safely. Key Tip: Keep track of which screw goes with which blade since they may differ slightly in length. Step 3: Check for Loose Parts Gently pull down on each fan blade to check if there is any play or wobble. If you find loose blades, tighten their connecting screws securely back into place. Also, look out for any other visible signs of wear like frayed wires or damaged parts. Step 4: Lubricate the Bearing Most modern fans come equipped with ball bearings inside the motor casing. Over time, these bearings can dry out causing friction and leading to the squeaky noise. To solve this issue, we need to re-lubricate them. Locate the opening for the grease fitting - often marked by a small hole near the center of the bottom part of the motor housing. Spray your chosen lubricant directly into this hole until you see a little bit seeping out from around the edges. Be careful not to overdo it; too much oil might attract dust and dirt, potentially damaging the motor. Guideline: Use light lubricants specifically designed for machinery such as WD-40 Specialist Grease or white lithium grease. Avoid using heavy oils or penetrating sprays as these could damage the motor windings. Step 5: Wipe Away Excess Oil After applying the lubricant, use a clean cloth or paper towel to remove excess oil drips from the exterior surface of the motor housing. Step 6: Reattach Blades Once everything looks good, reverse the disassembly process by replacing the fan blades onto the motor housing and securing them tightly with their respective screws. Make sure all screws are snug but not overtightened. Step 7: Test Run Your Fan Restore power to your ceiling fan via the wall switch and circuit breaker. Gradually increase the speed setting to observe whether the squeaking has ceased. It should now operate silently unless the noise was due to something else entirely unrelated to lack of lubrication. Conclusion: By following these steps meticulously, you've successfully diagnosed and treated one common cause behind a squeaking ceiling fan. Remember, regular maintenance checks help maintain optimal performance and longevity of appliances. So don't hesitate to repeat this procedure periodically even if your fan seems fine. Now I look in the mirror 'n' poo, poo boo. [these are both excuses for not cleaning the car.] Direct Proportions: A Simple Concept with Everyday Usage Have you ever heard of direct proportion before? It's actually quite a simple concept that we encounter every day without even realizing it! Let me give you an example. Imagine you have a lemonade stand and you sell each cup of lemonade for $0.50. Now, suppose you want to know how many cups of lemonade you can sell if you earn $20. To find out, you simply divide the total amount of money you made ($20) by the price per cup ($0.50). The answer is 40 cups. In other words, the number of cups (x) and the amount of money earned (r) are directly proportional because when one increases, so does the other. We write this relationship as x : r = 40 : 20 or simplified as x = r / 2. Now let's say you want to know how many cups of lemonade you can sell for $25 instead. You just substitute $25 into the equation and get x = 25 / 2 = 12.5 cups. But wait, you can't sell half a cup of lemonade! This shows us that while the relationship between the two variables is still direct, there may be restrictions based on real-world limitations. Nonetheless, understanding direct proportion allows you to predict trends and relationships between quantities easily. In algebraic terms, if two variables are directly proportional, their ratio remains constant regardless of the values they take. Using our previous example, the ratio x : r always equals 40 : 20 or simplified to 1 : 2. So if x = 5 when r = 40, then the same ratio applies when r changes value - meaning that x will equal 3.125 when r = 25 since 25 / 8 = 3.125. So next time you go shopping or plan your allowance, remember the idea of direct proportion. Understanding these basic mathematical concepts can make managing money and solving problems easier and more fun! I'm um the Punky Wok my pet Wok. [D leaves S to coast, snaps as he opens door, 12 sec, squeaks, murmurs] Imagine you have a bunch of your favorite candies lined up on a table, each one taking up a certain amount of space. Let's say these candies represent our intervals, and the space they take up on the table represents their length. Now, imagine you want to find all the candies that fit inside a specific container - this container is like our query interval. To make things easier, let's sort the candies based on where they start on the table. This will help us quickly identify which candies could possibly fit into our container. We can do this by creating something called a Segment Tree! A Segment Tree is like having many containers of different sizes, where each container knows which smaller containers it contains. In our candy example, we create a Segment Tree with containers representing various lengths, and each container stores information about the candies that fit perfectly within its size. Now, when we want to find candies that fit in our query container, we simply check the Segment Tree for containers that match or are larger than our query container. Since every container knows its subcontainers, we efficiently discover all the candies that fit perfectly inside our query container. This method is faster because instead of checking each candy individually, we first narrow down our options significantly using the sorted order and precomputed information stored in the Segment Tree. Once we know which containers in the Segment Tree might work, we just need to confirm whether the candies inside them indeed fit perfectly within our query container - no need to measure every single candy! So, next time you play with toys, think about how smartly organizing them according to their properties can make finding specific combinations a breeze!
